questions.question: What is the primary goal of Bayesian learning in machine learning?
options: A) To minimize the loss function
B) To maximize the likelihood function
C) To make predictions based on prior knowledge
D) To reduce the dimensionality of data
questions.explanation: Bayesian learning aims to update the prior knowledge with new data to make predictions or decisions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key concept in Bayes Theorem?
options: A) Independence
B) Conditional probability
C) Probability density function
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Bayes Theorem deals with conditional probability, which is a fundamental concept in Bayesian learning.

questions.question: What is the difference between a priori and posteriori probability?
options: A) Priori probability is the probability before observing data, while posteriori probability is after observing data
B) Priori probability is after observing data, while posteriori probability is before
C) Priori and posteriori probabilities are the same
D) Priori and posteriori probabilities are mutually exclusive
questions.explanation: Priori probability refers to the probability before observing data, while posteriori probability refers to the updated probability after observing data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of normalization in Bayesian learning?
options: A) To reduce the dimensionality of data
B) To increase the accuracy of predictions
C) To normalize the probability density function
D) To improve model interpretability
questions.explanation: Normalization is used to ensure that the probability density function integrates to 1, making it easier to interpret and compare different models.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common application of Bayesian learning?
options: A) Image recognition
B) Text classification
C) Recommendation systems
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Bayesian learning has been successfully applied in various areas, including image recognition, text classification, and recommendation systems.
